perturbation.original_text: The obligation of non-disclosure shall not extend to: (1) information which is then already in the possession of the Recipient and not under a duty of non- disclosure; (2) information which is generally known or revealed to the public other than by breach of this Agreement; (3) information which is revealed to the Recipient by a third party--unless such party is under a duty of non-disclosure; or (4) information which the Recipient develops independently of the di sclosure as indicated by files existing at the time of initial disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligation of non-disclosure shall not extend to: (1) information which is then already in the possession of the Recipient; (2) information which is generally known or revealed to the public other than by breach of this Agreement; (3) information which is revealed to the Recipient by a third party; or (4) information which the Recipient develops independently of the disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'and not under a duty of non-disclosure' from clause (1) and '--unless such party is under a duty of non-disclosure' from clause (3) weakens these exceptions and creates an in-text contradiction. It implies that information already possessed with a non-disclosure duty or received from a third party *with* a non-disclosure duty is now excluded from the Confidential Information definition, thus creating a loophole where non-disclosure requirements are lessened.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligation of non-disclosure shall not extend to: (1) information which is then already in the possession of the Recipient; (2) information which is generally known or revealed to the public other than by breach of this Agreement; (3) information which is revealed to the Recipient by a third party; or (4) information which the Recipient develops independently of the disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits 'and not under a duty of non-disclosure' from clause (1) and '--unless such party is under a duty of non-disclosure' from clause (3). This creates a contradiction because, under the original text, information already possessed under a non-disclosure agreement or received from a third party also bound by non-disclosure would be considered confidential. The changed text removes this protection, implying that such information is now exempt from non-disclosure obligations, directly conflicting with the original intent and potentially other clauses relying on a broader definition of confidential information. As the Changed Text and the Contradicted Text are the same, it is a direct in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event Recipient receives a subpoena or other validly issued administrative or judicial process requesting Confidential Information, Recipient shall provide prompt actual notice to the General Counsel of NASDAQ OMX of such receipt, providing the Corporations with a reasonable opportunity to intervene in the proceeding before the time that Recipient is required to comply with such subpoena or other process. In the event that NASDAQ OMX instructs Recipient not to comply with any portion or all of the process, Recipient shall comply with such instructions and NASDAQ OMX shall indemnify and hold harmless the Recipient from any damages to th e extent that Recipient's damages result from its compliance with NASDAQ OMX's instructions.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event Recipient receives a subpoena or other validly issued administrative or judicial process requesting Confidential Information, Recipient shall provide prompt actual notice to the General Counsel of NASDAQ OMX of such receipt, providing the Corporations with a reasonable opportunity to intervene in the proceeding before the time that Recipient is required to comply with such subpoena or other process. In the event that NASDAQ OMX instructs Recipient not to comply with any portion or all of the process, Recipient shall comply with such instructions.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and NASDAQ OMX shall indemnify and hold harmless the Recipient from any damages to th e extent that Recipient's damages result from its compliance with NASDAQ OMX's instructions.', it creates a contradiction by instructing the recipient to follow instructions of NASDAQ OMX, but then the text doesn't guarantee the indemnity of the receiver of that information if there is an instruction given to them to not comply. This lack of indemnity creates risk to the Recipient for following the instructions, because the liability is not clearly defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event Recipient receives a subpoena or other validly issued administrative or judicial process requesting Confidential Information, Recipient shall provide prompt actual notice to the General Counsel of NASDAQ OMX of such receipt, providing the Corporations with a reasonable opportunity to intervene in the proceeding before the time that Recipient is required to comply with such subpoena or other process. In the event that NASDAQ OMX instructs Recipient not to comply with any portion or all of the process, Recipient shall comply with such instructions.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that NASDAQ OMX will indemnify the Recipient if the Recipient follows NASDAQ OMX's instructions regarding the subpoena. The changed text removes the indemnification clause, instructing the Recipient to comply with NASDAQ OMX's instructions, but removing the guarantee of indemnification. This creates a contradiction, as the Recipient is still instructed to comply, but is no longer guaranteed to be held harmless from damages resulting from that compliance. The contradicted text also involves the Recipient complying with NASDAQ OMX instructions, highlighting the created contradiction because the change makes the outcome of compliance uncertain regarding liability, directly conflicting with the previous guarantee and obligation.
